name: NPS Calculator
description: Estimate growth of National Pension Scheme investments.

example.explanation: NPS investments grow with compounding returns over the investment period.

faqs.question: How is Gratuity calculated?
faqs.answer: 15 days salary × completed years (max ₹20L). Requires minimum 5 years service.

faqs.question: Can I withdraw EPF before retirement?
faqs.answer: Partial withdrawal at 50+ years or for specific reasons (home, education, medical). Full withdrawal at age 60.

faqs.question: Which is better: NPS vs PPF?
faqs.answer: NPS: flexible investor choice, open-ended, tax deduction. PPF: fixed returns, simpler, lower risk.

faqs.question: What's the NPS contribution limit?
faqs.answer: Additional tax benefit (80CCD(1B)): ₹50K/year. Through employer: 14% of salary covered in 80C.

faqs.question: Is EPF money really yours?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's your account. Employer contribution (12%) + your 8% = total corpus. Government doesn't take it.

faqs.question: What's the VPF lock-in period?
faqs.answer: VPF contribution locked till 5 years or retirement, whichever is earlier. Interest rate same as regular EPF.

concept: Government scheme calculators help you plan for retirements, benefits, and mandated payouts under Indian laws (Gratuity, EPF, NPS, PPF).
guideSteps: Enter your salary, tenure, or contribution details as required.
Input your age and expected retirement age if relevant.
Select the scheme or benefit type.
Click Calculate to see your projected payout or corpus.
Note down the results for your retirement and financial planning.
standards: Gratuity: ₹15 days salary × completed years (max ₹20 lakhs)
EPF: Both employer and employee contribute 12% (employee gets 8-12% match)
NPS: You contribute to a personal retirement account; min ₹500/month.
PPF: 15-year scheme with 7.1-7.25% interest, tax-free maturity.
VPF: Can contribute up to 100% of salary; rolled into regular EPF.
